mainText: Good morning. Here is your AI briefing. We have a busy day of updates across the industry. Starting with OpenAI, the company has officially launched ChatGPT Work, a new agent designed to act as a partner for your most ambitious projects. By integrating directly with your apps and files, it can stay with a task for hours and transform your goals into finished outputs. OpenAI is already highlighting specific use cases for this, noting how sales teams can leverage it for pipeline briefs and account planning, while data science teams can use it to generate KPI memos and technical dashboard specifications. Next, Microsoft 365 Copilot has received a significant upgrade. OpenAI announced that GPT-5.6 is now the preferred model powering the suite, bringing stronger AI capabilities to Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and beyond to ensure faster and higher-quality work for users. In the telecommunications sector, OpenAI shared insights into how Deutsche Telekom is undergoing a major transformation to become an AI-native company. They are currently utilizing OpenAI’s technology to revamp their customer service, streamline internal employee workflows, and innovate their network operations and voice services. Moving to the education sector, Google DeepMind has partnered with the Atal Innovation Mission to launch ATL Saathi. This is a new, Gemini-powered tool specifically designed to empower educators working in robotics labs across India, helping the next generation of innovators get hands-on experience with advanced AI technology. Finally, OpenAI is taking a critical look at how we measure AI progress. They recently published an analysis pointing out reliability and accuracy concerns within the popular SWE-Bench Pro coding benchmark. By identifying the difference between signal and noise in these evaluations, OpenAI is pushing for more rigorous standards when it comes to testing the capabilities of frontier AI models. That is your AI briefing for today.
